projects.title: Morse Code to Eye Blink Decoder for Assistive Communication Web App
projects.description: Developed an assistive communication system that translates Morse code generated through eye blinks into natural language text, enabling hands-free communication for individuals with severe speech and motor impairments.

projects.title: Weapon Detection with Vision-Language Model Validation for Intelligent Surveillance
projects.description: Undergraduate thesis research focused on improving weapon detection reliability by integrating object detection models with Vision-Language Models (VLMs). Employed a two-stage pipeline where a detector identifies potential weapons, and a VLM performs contextual validation and reasoning to verify detections, reducing false positives.

projects.title: SehatMOK: Makan Olah Kelola – Smart Nutrition & Food Inventory Platform
projects.description: A health and nutrition management platform helping users track daily nutrition, manage food inventory, discover AI-generated recipes from available ingredients, plan meals, monitor hydration, and organize grocery lists. Integrated Google Gemini AI for recipe generation.

projects.title: EcoHike: Automatic Trash Monitoring System on Hiking Trail Mobile Application
projects.description: Offline-first mobile system for automatic waste detection on mountain hiking trails using YOLOv12. Enables real-time object detection directly on smartphones for hands-free field operation via chest strap without internet connectivity.

projects.title: AI-Driven Credit Risk Analysis for Microfinance and MSMEs in Indonesia
projects.description: Led a data mining team analyzing credit risk patterns in Indonesia's microfinance and MSME (UMKM) sector, using AI-based approaches to identify risk factors.

projects.title: A Comparative Analysis of YOLOv12 and RF-DETR for Weapon Detection in CCTV Footage
projects.description: Research exploring transformer-based RF-DETR against CNN-based YOLOv12 variants for weapon detection on CCTV surveillance footage. Published on IEEE Xplore, 5th ICSINTESA (November 2025).

projects.title: MripatAI: Eye-Blink-Based Morse Code Communication with Adaptive Decoding and NLP-Driven Error Correction
projects.description: Assistive communication system translating eye blinks to Morse code using YOLOv12-cls for eye classification, adaptive threshold calibration, and IndoBERT for error correction.

projects.title: System of a Road AI (SoaR AI) – Multimodal AI for Smart City Safety
projects.description: AI-powered web-based system detecting traffic accidents, emergency vehicles, and fire incidents in real-time. Integrates YOLOv12-M for computer vision and CRNN for audio recognition.

projects.title: Implementation of LightGBM for Predicting Formula Milk Sales Based on Sales Database
projects.description: Sales prediction using LightGBM for inventory planning. Placed #4 in Machine Learning Quest.

projects.title: Implementation of MobileViTV2 as an Efficient and Affordable Solution for Diagnosing the Severity Level of Diabetic Retinopathy
projects.description: Implemented MobileViTV2 to classify diabetic retinopathy severity from retinal images for Datathon UI.

projects.title: Riveria: AI Innovation Based on IoT and Computer Vision for Waste Collection and Sorting in Rivers
projects.description: AI-powered environmental solution integrating IoT and computer vision using YOLOv12 and sensors. Top 200 in Samsung Solve for Tomorrow.

projects.title: Assistify – Digital Personal Assistant Web Application
projects.description: Web productivity application containing text & voice notes, to-do list, reminders, alarm, and journal.

posts.content:
As my undergraduate journey almost comes to an end, I want to take a moment to express my gratitude to everyone who trusted me with opportunities to learn and grow outside the classroom.
A special thank you to Himpunan Mahasiswa Informatika for entrusting me with various events throughout my university years. From serving as a Division Staff in Carnival 2024 and FORTRAN 2025 to being trusted twice as a Division Head in Carnival 2025 Vol. 1 and Vol. 2, each experience taught me valuable lessons in leadership, teamwork, responsibility, and commitment.
I am also grateful to the Multimedia Laboratory for trusting me to become the Master of Ceremony for GameJAMM 2026, something I had never done before. What started as an unfamiliar challenge became one of the most memorable opportunities that pushed me beyond my comfort zone and helped me grow both personally and professionally.
Thank you to everyone who believed in me and gave me the chance to contribute. As this chapter comes to a close, I will carry these experiences, lessons, and memories with me into the next stage of my journey.
#Grateful
 
#Leadership
 
#StudentLife
 
#TelkomUniversity

I’m excited to share that our paper “A Comparative Analysis of YOLOv12 and RF-DETR for Weapon Detection in CCTV Footage” has been published on IEEE Xplore by the IEEE.
This work explores the challenge of automated weapon detection in CCTV surveillance, where occlusion, varying viewpoints, and low-resolution imagery often impact detection performance. We compared YOLOv12 variants with the transformer-based RF-DETR model using challenging CCTV footage and evaluated generalization on an unseen camera viewpoint.
The results show that RF-DETR outperforms all YOLOv12 variants, highlighting the stronger robustness of transformer-based architectures for real-world surveillance scenarios.
Special thanks to the team at the AI Laboratory for the collaboration and support throughout this research.
 
Muhammad Syafiq Aqilla
 
Arnoldus Bryan Hendry
 
Muhammad Rafly Arjasubrata
Our paper is available here:
 
https://lnkd.in/g7seGJpK
#ComputerVision
 
#DeepLearning
 
#ObjectDetection
 
#AIResearch
 
#IEEE
 
#YOLO
 
#Transformers
 
#PublicSafety

Grateful to share that I've received this Certificate of Appreciation for being a Top 50 Finalist in "DigiHack 2025: AI for Good - Empowering Innovation with Telkom Group."
The theme "AI for Good" is particularly meaningful, and it was inspiring to be part of a national competition dedicated to creating impactful solutions. This was a challenging and rewarding experience spanning from August to October.
A huge thank you to Telkom Indonesia and Digistar Club for organizing this event and fostering a community of innovators.
#AIforGood
 
#DigiHack2025
 
#TelkomIndonesia
 
#DigistarClub
 
#SocialImpact
 
#Innovation
 
#ArtificialIntelligence
 
#Hackathon
 
#Top50

Feeling incredibly grateful and excited for what's ahead!
I'm thrilled to announce that I have been entrusted to continue my role as a Researcher at the Focus Group of AI Laboratory Telkom University for the 2025/2026 academic year. A huge thank you to the lab for the continued trust and support.
This news comes as my team and I celebrate a key achievement from my tenure in 2024/2025. Our research paper, "A Comparative Analysis of YOLOv12 and RF-DETR for Weapon Detection in CCTV Footage," has been accepted for publication at the 2025 5th International Conference of Science and Information Technology in Smart Administration (ICSINTESA), an IEEE-sponsored conference.
In this work, we explored and compared cutting-edge AI models to enhance public safety through automated weapon detection in surveillance systems. This accomplishment would not have been possible without my brilliant co-authors:
 
Muhammad Syafiq Aqilla
,
 
Arnoldus Bryan Hendry
,
 
Muhammad Rafly Arjasubrata
, and our esteemed mentor, Mr. Mahmud Dwi Sulistiyo.
Deepest gratitude to AI Laboratory and Telkom University for providing the resources and environment to conduct impactful research. Looking forward to another year of learning and innovation!

#Research

#AcademicExcellence

🎉 Proud to be a Finalist in the Intelligent Expression & Computational Thinking for Research and Application (INTELLECTRA) 2025 for Machine Learning Quest (MLQ) category — one of the most competitive and prestigious data-driven challenges in Indonesia!
Alongside my incredible team, Tim Anomali, we tackled complex financial datasets and built quantitative models that pushed our analytical skills to the next level. Reaching the finals among top talents from across the country has been an exciting journey full of learning and innovation.
Grateful to the organizers and mentors, and looking forward to applying what we’ve learned to real-world problems. Onward and upward! 🚀📊
#DataScience
 
#CompetitionFinalist
 
#TeamAnomali
 
#LearningNeverStops

📸 Capturing Moments, Creating Memories 🎓
As the Head of Documentation & Publication for Carnival 2025, I had the incredible opportunity to lead a team of talented photographers in preserving the essence of the event. From fleeting expressions to grand celebrations, every snapshot tells a story—one that reflects the spirit and energy of our campus community.
Grateful for the teamwork, the vision, and the passion that fueled this project. Here's to the power of storytelling through the lens! 📷✨
#Photography
 
#CampusEvent
 
#StorytellingThroughPhotos
 
#Leadership
 
#Teamwork
